factors.summary: The company expects capital spending between $125 million and $135 million for 2019, including $20 million related to the ASP acquisition.

evidence.text: For the full year 2019, we expect capital spending to be between approximately $125 million and $135 million, which includes approximately $20 million of one-time capital expenditures related to the integration of the ASP acquisition.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is investing in capital expenditures, which can drive future growth.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation increased year-over-year, primarily due to operating activities.

evidence.text: Cash flows from operating activities were approximately $800 million during the first nine months of 2019, an increase of $22 million compared to 2018.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Free cash flow increased year-over-year, indicating strong cash generation.

factors.summary: The company has increased its debt, including the issuance of convertible senior notes and a delayed-draw term loan, but maintains liquidity through credit facilities.

evidence.text: Financing activities generated cash of $2.6 billion during the nine months ended September 27, 2019, of which $1.4 billion was due to the issuance of our 0.875% senior convertible notes and $1.0 billion was related to the issuance of our 364-day delayed-draw term loan facility due in 2020.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: While debt has increased, the company maintains liquidity, which is a balanced view.

factors.summary: Input costs are rising due to inflationary pressures and tariffs.

evidence.text: Increased material costs associated primarily with inflationary pressures and recently enacted tariffs.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Rising input costs due to inflation and tariffs are a significant headwind.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are compressing due to unfavorable factors.

evidence.text: The respective 170 basis point and 190 basis point decrease in gross profit margins for the three and nine months ended September 27, 2019, as compared to the comparable periods in 2018, is due primarily to unfavorable sales mix, acquisition-related fair value adjustments to deferred revenue and inventory, and increased material costs associated primarily with inflationary pressures and recently enacted tariffs.
evidence.subsection: COST OF SALES AND GROSS PROFIT

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Compressed margins due to unfavorable factors are a concern.

factors.summary: The Industrial Technologies segment experienced growth, particularly in transportation technologies due to EMV standards, while the Professional Instrumentation segment faced slowing macroeconomic conditions.

evidence.text: In our Industrial Technologies segment, the liability shift related to enhanced credit card security requirements for outdoor payment systems that is expected to occur in October 2020 in the United States based on the Europay, Mastercard and Visa ('EMV') global standards is continuing to drive demand within our transportation technologies platform. The decline in our Professional Instrumentation segment reflects slowing macroeconomic conditions across most major markets.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed performance across segments; growth in one area but decline in another.

factors.summary: Management expects overall sales to continue growing year-over-year but anticipates sales from existing businesses to be relatively flat.

evidence.text: While we expect overall sales to continue to grow on a year-over-year basis during the remainder of 2019, we expect sales from existing businesses to be relatively flat as we continue to monitor macro-economic developments and the corresponding impact on our businesses.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Overall sales expected to grow, but existing businesses may see flat sales, indicating a mixed outlook.

factors.summary: Fortive is investing in new products and services, particularly in high-growth markets, which is contributing to their sales growth.

evidence.text: Our businesses design, develop, service, manufacture and market professional and engineered products, software and services for a variety of end markets, building upon leading brand names, innovative technology and significant market positions.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Investment in new products and services in high-growth markets is a positive development.

factors.summary: Fortive has been actively involved in M&A activities, including significant acquisitions and a planned separation, which have negatively impacted operating profit margins due to dilutive effects.

evidence.text: The incremental year-over-year net dilutive effect of acquired businesses — unfavorable 415 basis points.
On April 1, 2019, we acquired the advanced sterilization products business ('ASP') of Johnson & Johnson...
On September 4, 2019, we announced our intention to separate into two independent, publicly traded companies...

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: The M&A activities have negatively impacted operating profit margins due to dilutive effects.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth appears to be mixed, with some segments showing slight decreases or flat performance, while others exhibit stable or modest growth.

evidence.text: Sales from existing businesses in our Professional Instrumentation segment’s Advanced Instrumentation & Solutions businesses decreased at a low-single digit rate during the three months ended September 27, 2019 and were relatively flat during the nine months ended September 27, 2019 as compared to the comparable periods of 2018.

On a year-over-year basis for the three months ended September 27, 2019, our Industrial Technologies segment reported sales growth from existing businesses of 6.5%, while our Professional Instrumentation segment reported a decline in sales from existing businesses of 1.3%.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Organic growth is mixed with some segments declining and others growing modestly.

factors.summary: Demand trends are mixed, with some areas showing growth and others experiencing declines. While overall demand for Fortive's products and services increased, leading to a 16.2% year-over-year total sales growth, there were specific declines in certain product categories and regions.

evidence.text: Year-over-year sales from existing businesses of field solutions products and services declined at a low-single digit rate during the three months ended September 27, 2019, as growth in demand for portable gas detection and facilities maintenance and asset management offerings was more than offset by declines in demand for electrical grid condition-based monitoring equipment and slowing demand from our industrial channel partners in North America.

While differences exist among our businesses, on an overall basis, demand for our products, software and services increased during the three months ended September 27, 2019 as compared to the comparable period of 2018 resulting in aggregate year-over-year total sales growth of 16.2% and sales growth from existing businesses of 2.1% .

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Overall demand increased, leading to significant total sales growth despite some declines.
